title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jervis proposes a hypothesis to explain the discrepancies surrounding Helen Kennedy's death. He suggests that Calvin Harriott did not actually kill his wife, but was instead manipulated into believing he had. According to Jervis, an unknown individual, referred to as "X," could have anesthetized Harriott by spiking his whisky with a high concentration of anesthetic. While Harriott was unconscious, X allegedly strangled Helen in the hall, moved her to a bed to simulate a romantic encounter, and then relocated the body. This theory would explain why Harriott woke up in a state of confusion and jealousy, believing he had committed the crime.

The group discusses potential motives for "X," focusing on men from Helen's past. Miss Marple reveals that Helen had previously been engaged to a lawyer named Walter Feen, whom she traveled abroad to marry before breaking the engagement. Miss Marple notes that Feen is now a senior partner at a law firm and lives with his mother. Gwendolyn suggests that Helen may have encountered another man while traveling abroad, possibly a married man of high social standing. Miss Marple supports this possibility, suggesting that a passionate but impossible romance could have been the reason Helen broke her engagement to Feen and returned home. Gwendolyn concludes that Helen's subsequent relationship with her father was not a passionate affair, but rather a mutual consolation between two people sharing personal troubles.
